title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about doing exercise.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: You said you wanted to get fitter. Have you joined a gym? || Man: Not yet. The nearest one isn't too expensive, and there are good sports facilities close to my flat. || Woman: So what's stopping you? || Man: [8]I leave home at seven and often don't get back until after eight, so there just aren't many free evenings[/8]. || Woman: Could you do something at lunchtime? || Man: Maybe. A short walk would be better than nothing. || Woman: Good idea. || Man: I'll do that, and then I won't have to worry about it again.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: You said you wanted to get fitter. Have you joined a gym?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not yet. The nearest one isn't too expensive, and there are good sports facilities close to my flat.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what's stopping you?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I leave home at seven and often don't get back until after eight, so there just aren't many free evenings.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Could you do something at lunchtime?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe. A short walk would be better than nothing.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good idea.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I'll do that, and then I won't have to worry about it again.

questions.question: Why is the man finding it difficult to do exercise?

options.A: He doesn't have a lot of free time.
options.B: He can't afford to go to the gym.
options.C: There aren't any sports facilities nearby.

questions.explanation: He says he leaves early and comes home late, so he has little free time.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking in a restaurant.
extracts.audio_script: Man: What did you think of the meal? The soup was unusual. || Woman: I liked it, though I wouldn't call it spicy. The garlic bread was the best part for me. || Man: My main course was excellent. [9]That fish was cooked perfectly, with lots of herbs and lemon[/9]. || Woman: I agree. I tasted a bit of yours, and it was much better than my pasta. || Man: The dessert looked tiny, though. || Woman: Mine was small, but very rich.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What did you think of the meal? The soup was unusual.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I liked it, though I wouldn't call it spicy. The garlic bread was the best part for me.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: My main course was excellent. That fish was cooked perfectly, with lots of herbs and lemon.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. I tasted a bit of yours, and it was much better than my pasta.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The dessert looked tiny, though.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Mine was small, but very rich.

questions.question: They agree that

options.B: the fish dish was very tasty.
options.C: one of the desserts was too small.

questions.explanation: They both say the fish was very good.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman telling her friend about her neighbours.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How are your new neighbours? || Woman: They're friendly enough. They always smile when I see them, and noise isn't really a problem. || Man: That's lucky. || Woman: Yes, but there's one thing. [10]Since they moved in, they've left old boxes and bags beside the front door, and the hall looks terrible[/10]. || Man: Could you mention it politely? || Woman: I think I will. I don't want to start badly with them. || Man: That makes sense. || Woman: Exactly, and I feel much happier about it now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How are your new neighbours?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: They're friendly enough. They always smile when I see them, and noise isn't really a problem.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's lucky.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but there's one thing. Since they moved in, they've left old boxes and bags beside the front door, and the hall looks terrible.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Could you mention it politely?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I think I will. I don't want to start badly with them.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly, and I feel much happier about it now.

questions.question: What problem does she have with her neighbours?
questions.explanation: She says they leave boxes and bags in the hall, making it look terrible.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new museum.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you visited the new transport museum? || Man: Yes, it's fantastic. Did you go at the weekend? || Woman: I did, but only for an hour because it closed earlier than I expected. The old buses were interesting, though not surprising because I'd seen similar ones before. || Man: Will you go back? || Woman: [11]Definitely. I didn't have time to see the upstairs rooms, so I'd like another visit soon[/11]. || Man: Let me know. I'll come with you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you visited the new transport museum?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, it's fantastic. Did you go at the weekend?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did, but only for an hour because it closed earlier than I expected. The old buses were interesting, though not surprising because I'd seen similar ones before.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Will you go back?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I didn't have time to see the upstairs rooms, so I'd like another visit soon.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Let me know. I'll come with you.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the museum?

options.A: She hopes to have another chance to visit.
options.C: She was surprised by some things on display.

questions.explanation: She says she wants another visit because she did not have time to see everything.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking to a colleague about a hotel he stayed in.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How was your hotel in Manchester? || Man: Comfortable, and the room was big enough for me. It was close to the station too. || Woman: That sounds good. || Man: Mostly, yes, but [12]the website said it was in a quiet side street, and actually my window faced a busy road with buses passing all night[/12]. || Woman: Oh no, so you didn't sleep well? || Man: Not really. I'd choose somewhere else next time. || Woman: I'm sure that will help. || Man: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How was your hotel in Manchester?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Comfortable, and the room was big enough for me. It was close to the station too.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds good.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Mostly, yes, but the website said it was in a quiet side street, and actually my window faced a busy road with buses passing all night.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Oh no, so you didn't sleep well?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. I'd choose somewhere else next time.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'm sure that will help.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

questions.question: The man complains that

options.A: the room was too small for him.
options.B: the location wasn't what he expected.
options.C: he was disturbed by the traffic.

questions.explanation: He says his window faced a busy road with buses passing all night, so traffic disturbed him.

extracts.context: You will hear two passengers talking on an aeroplane.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is this your first flight? || Woman: No, but I don't fly often. I usually take the train because I can move around more easily. || Man: I fly every month for work. It's the quickest way for me. || Woman: I understand that. [13]For this journey flying is more convenient, but I still find trains much more relaxing[/13]. || Man: I know what you mean. Seats on planes can feel small. || Woman: Exactly. I'll be happy when we land.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is this your first flight?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: No, but I don't fly often. I usually take the train because I can move around more easily.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I fly every month for work. It's the quickest way for me.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I understand that. For this journey flying is more convenient, but I still find trains much more relaxing.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I know what you mean. Seats on planes can feel small.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. I'll be happy when we land.

questions.question: How does the woman feel about flying?

options.A: She thinks it is always frightening.
options.B: She prefers travelling by train to flying.
options.C: She finds it a relaxing way to travel.

questions.explanation: She says flying is convenient for this journey, but trains are much more relaxing.
